seoTitle: Genuway Crystal Oscillators and Resonators | Authorized Distributor
seoDescription: Browse Genuway crystal products: crystal resonators, oscillators, automotive crystals, differential oscillators, RTC crystals. In stock, technical support.

faqs.question: What are the main product categories offered by Genuway?
faqs.answer: Genuway offers five core product categories: (1) Crystal Resonators - Passive quartz crystals in various packages from HC-49/S to compact SMD; (2) Crystal Oscillators - Active timing solutions including SPXO, TCXO, and OCXO with various stability options; (3) Automotive Crystals - AEC-Q200 qualified devices for automotive electronics; (4) Differential Oscillators - Low-jitter oscillators with LVDS, LVPECL outputs for high-speed communications; (5) RTC Crystals - 32.768kHz tuning fork crystals for real-time clock applications. Each category offers multiple series optimized for specific applications and performance requirements.
faqs.decisionGuide: Click on any product category to explore detailed specifications, available models, and application guidance.

faqs.question: How do I choose between crystal resonators and crystal oscillators?
faqs.answer: The choice depends on your design requirements and resources: Choose Crystal Resonators when you have oscillator circuitry available in your design, cost is a primary concern, and you have the expertise to design the oscillator circuit. Resonators are lower cost but require external components. Choose Crystal Oscillators when you need guaranteed startup and stable operation, want simplified design with integrated oscillator circuit, require better frequency stability (TCXO/OCXO), or need differential outputs for high-speed signals. Oscillators are plug-and-play solutions that save design time and reduce risk. For most modern designs, oscillators are preferred unless cost is extremely critical.
faqs.decisionGuide: Browse our crystal resonators or oscillators categories to compare specifications and find the optimal solution for your timing requirements.

faqs.question: What factors should I consider when selecting crystal frequency stability?
faqs.answer: Frequency stability selection depends on your application requirements: Standard Stability (±10 to ±50ppm) - Suitable for general-purpose applications like microcontrollers, consumer electronics; TCXO (±0.5 to ±2.5ppm) - Required for communications, GPS, networking equipment where better stability is needed; OCXO (±0.005 to ±0.1ppm) - Essential for base stations, test equipment, satellite communications requiring highest precision. Consider: Operating Temperature Range - Wider ranges require better stability grades; Aging - Long-term drift requirements; Phase Noise - Critical for RF and high-speed applications. Higher stability comes with higher cost and power consumption (especially OCXO).
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our TCXO and OCXO categories for high-stability applications, or contact our FAE team for stability requirements analysis.

faqs.question: Which Genuway products are suitable for automotive applications?
faqs.answer: Genuway offers AEC-Q200 qualified products specifically designed for automotive applications: Automotive Crystal Resonators - HC-49/S and SMD packages qualified to AEC-Q200 with extended temperature range; Automotive Oscillators - SPXO and TCXO with automotive qualification; Features include: Extended Temperature Range - -40°C to +125°C or higher; Enhanced Reliability - Rigorous testing for automotive environmental stresses; Full Traceability - Lot traceability for automotive quality management; Long-term Supply - Production continuity guaranteed for automotive lifecycle. These products are ideal for ADAS, infotainment systems, powertrain control, battery management, and body electronics in modern vehicles.
faqs.decisionGuide: Look for automotive-qualified products in each category, or contact our automotive FAE specialist for recommendations.

faqs.question: What are the advantages of differential oscillators over single-ended oscillators?
faqs.answer: Differential oscillators offer significant advantages for high-performance applications: Lower Jitter - Differential signaling reduces noise and provides cleaner clock signals essential for high-speed communications; Better EMI Performance - Differential signals generate less electromagnetic interference and are more immune to external noise; Higher Frequency Capability - Differential outputs support higher frequencies required by modern communications standards; Standard Interfaces - LVDS, LVPECL, and HCSL outputs compatible with high-speed ICs; Signal Integrity - Differential transmission maintains signal quality over longer traces. Applications include: 5G/6G base stations, optical networking, high-speed data converters, FPGA clocking, and SerDes applications. The trade-off is higher cost and power consumption compared to single-ended CMOS oscillators.
faqs.decisionGuide: Explore our Differential Oscillators category for high-speed communications applications requiring low jitter.

categories.name: Crystal Oscillators
categories.fullName: Genuway Crystal Oscillators | SPXO, TCXO, OCXO
categories.description: High-precision crystal oscillators providing stable clock signals for communications, computing, and industrial applications.
categories.longDescription:
Genuway crystal oscillators provide complete integrated timing solutions with excellent frequency stability and low phase noise. The product line includes SPXO (Simple Packaged Crystal Oscillator) for general-purpose applications, TCXO (Temperature Compensated Crystal Oscillator) for improved stability across temperature variations, and OCXO (Oven Controlled Crystal Oscillator) for highest precision applications.

These oscillators feature CMOS, LVDS, LVPECL, and HCSL output options to interface with various system requirements. With stabilities ranging from ±50ppm (SPXO) to ±0.005ppm (OCXO), Genuway oscillators serve applications from consumer electronics to satellite communications.

All oscillators undergo rigorous testing including temperature cycling, shock, vibration, and aging tests to ensure long-term reliability. Automotive-grade options are available with AEC-Q200 qualification for demanding automotive applications.

series.description: Standard crystal oscillators for general-purpose timing applications

series.name: TCXO Series
series.description: Temperature compensated oscillators for improved stability

selectionGuide.title: How to Select the Right Crystal Oscillator
selectionGuide.description: Comprehensive guide to choosing the optimal crystal oscillator based on stability, frequency, and application requirements

faqs.question: What is the difference between SPXO, TCXO, and OCXO?
faqs.answer: SPXO (Simple Packaged Crystal Oscillator) provides basic oscillator function with typical stability of ±10 to ±50ppm. It uses a standard crystal without temperature compensation, making it cost-effective for general applications. TCXO (Temperature Compensated Crystal Oscillator) incorporates temperature compensation circuitry to achieve better stability of ±0.5 to ±2.5ppm across temperature variations. It uses a temperature sensor and compensation network to counteract crystal frequency drift. OCXO (Oven Controlled Crystal Oscillator) maintains the crystal at a constant elevated temperature using a precision oven, achieving the best stability of ±0.005 to ±0.1ppm. OCXOs are larger, consume more power, and are used in applications requiring highest precision like base stations and test equipment.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose SPXO for cost-sensitive applications, TCXO for improved stability requirements, and OCXO for highest precision applications.

faqs.question: How does temperature affect crystal oscillator frequency?
faqs.answer: Crystal oscillator frequency changes with temperature due to the crystal's temperature coefficient. AT-cut crystals, most commonly used, have a characteristic parabolic frequency vs temperature curve with turning points around 25°C and 80°C. Frequency deviation can be ±10 to ±50ppm over industrial temperature range (-40°C to +85°C) for standard crystals. TCXOs use compensation circuits to reduce this to ±0.5 to ±2.5ppm. OCXOs maintain constant crystal temperature, virtually eliminating temperature effects. When selecting oscillators, consider your operating temperature range and required stability. For wide temperature ranges with tight stability requirements, TCXO or OCXO is necessary.
faqs.decisionGuide: For wide temperature operation, select TCXO or OCXO based on your stability requirements, or contact our FAE team for guidance.

faqs.question: What is phase noise and why is it important?
faqs.answer: Phase noise measures the short-term frequency instability of an oscillator, representing noise sidebands around the carrier frequency. It's specified in dBc/Hz at various offset frequencies from the carrier. Lower phase noise (more negative dBc/Hz values) indicates cleaner spectral purity. Phase noise is critical in: RF Communications - Affects receiver sensitivity and selectivity; High-speed Digital - Impacts jitter and bit error rates; Radar Systems - Determines detection capability; Test Equipment - Limits measurement accuracy. Differential oscillators (LVDS/LVPECL) typically offer better phase noise than single-ended CMOS. For communications applications, phase noise at specific offset frequencies (like 1kHz, 10kHz, 100kHz from carrier) is often specified.
faqs.decisionGuide: For RF and high-speed applications, select oscillators with specified phase noise performance, or contact our FAE team for recommendations.

products.description: Standard crystal oscillator in compact 3.2x2.5mm package
products.longDescription:
The Genuway GXO-3225-25M is a high-quality SPXO (Simple Packaged Crystal Oscillator) providing a stable 25MHz clock output in a compact 3.2x2.5mm ceramic SMD package. This oscillator features CMOS output compatible with most digital circuits and operates from a standard 3.3V supply.

With frequency stability of ±30ppm over the commercial temperature range, this oscillator is ideal for general-purpose applications including microcontroller clocking, consumer electronics, and communication interfaces. The compact size makes it suitable for space-constrained designs while maintaining excellent reliability.
products.seoDescription: GXO-3225-25M - Genuway 25MHz crystal oscillator, 3.2x2.5mm, ±30ppm. In stock, same-day shipping. Contact: +86 15013702378
products.shortDescription: Compact 25MHz crystal oscillator in 3.2x2.5mm package with ±30ppm stability
descriptionParagraphs: The Genuway GXO-3225-25M delivers reliable 25MHz clock output in a space-saving 3.2x2.5mm package.
With ±30ppm stability and 3.3V CMOS output, this oscillator is perfect for microcontroller and consumer applications.
The compact ceramic package ensures excellent reliability and consistent performance.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Timing Products
faeReview.content: The GXO-3225-25M is my go-to recommendation for general-purpose 25MHz clocking applications. What I appreciate about this oscillator is its excellent price-performance ratio - it delivers reliable performance at a very competitive price point. I've successfully used this part in numerous microcontroller-based designs, communication interfaces, and consumer products. The 3.2x2.5mm package is a good balance between size and ease of handling during assembly. The ±30ppm stability is adequate for most digital applications, and the CMOS output interfaces easily with standard logic. For designers looking for a reliable, cost-effective 25MHz oscillator, this is an excellent choice.
faeReview.highlight: Excellent price-performance ratio for general-purpose 25MHz applications

alternativeParts.reason: Higher frequency for faster clocking requirements

faqs.question: What applications is the GXO-3225-25M best suited for?
faqs.answer: The GXO-3225-25M is optimized for general-purpose digital applications: Microcontroller Clocking - Provides stable clock for MCUs and processors; Communication Interfaces - UART, SPI, I2C clocking; Consumer Electronics - Set-top boxes, gaming devices, toys; Industrial Control - PLCs, industrial automation; General Digital - Any application requiring 25MHz clock. Key specifications matching these applications: 25MHz frequency common for many MCUs, ±30ppm stability adequate for digital circuits, 3.3V CMOS output compatible with standard logic, compact 3.2x2.5mm package for space-constrained designs.
faqs.decisionGuide: This oscillator is ideal for your general-purpose 25MHz clocking needs. Contact us for volume pricing and delivery schedules.

products.description: High-frequency crystal oscillator for faster clocking applications
products.longDescription:
The Genuway GXO-3225-50M is a high-frequency SPXO providing a stable 50MHz clock output in a compact 3.2x2.5mm ceramic SMD package. This oscillator doubles the frequency of the standard 25MHz part while maintaining the same compact footprint and reliability.

With frequency stability of ±30ppm and CMOS output, this oscillator is ideal for high-speed applications including Ethernet interfaces, high-performance microcontrollers, and communication systems. The 50MHz frequency is commonly used for 100Mbps Ethernet PHY clocking and other high-speed digital applications.
products.seoDescription: GXO-3225-50M - Genuway 50MHz crystal oscillator, 3.2x2.5mm, ±30ppm. In stock, same-day shipping. Contact: +86 15013702378
products.shortDescription: High-frequency 50MHz crystal oscillator in compact 3.2x2.5mm package
descriptionParagraphs: The Genuway GXO-3225-50M delivers reliable 50MHz clock output for high-speed applications.
With ±30ppm stability and 3.3V CMOS output, this oscillator is ideal for Ethernet and high-speed digital circuits.
The compact ceramic package maintains the same footprint as lower frequency options.

faeReview.author: Michael Chen
faeReview.title: Senior FAE - Timing Products
faeReview.content: The GXO-3225-50M is an excellent choice for applications requiring higher clock speeds. I've used this oscillator extensively for Ethernet PHY clocking, where the 50MHz frequency is standard for 100Mbps interfaces. The fact that it uses the same 3.2x2.5mm package as the 25MHz version provides flexibility in design - you can upgrade frequency without changing PCB layout. The stability and reliability are consistent with Genuway's quality standards. For any application requiring 50MHz clocking, this oscillator provides excellent value.
faeReview.highlight: Same package as 25MHz version allows flexible frequency selection

faqs.question: What applications require 50MHz clock frequency?
faqs.answer: The GXO-3225-50M is commonly used for: Ethernet PHY - 50MHz is standard clock for 100Mbps Ethernet physical layer; High-speed MCUs - Many high-performance microcontrollers use 50MHz clock; Communication Systems - Various communication interfaces requiring higher clock speeds; Video Processing - Some video and graphics applications use 50MHz reference; FPGA Clocking - General-purpose clocking for FPGA designs. The 50MHz frequency provides twice the clock rate of standard 25MHz parts, enabling higher data throughput and processing speeds.
faqs.decisionGuide: Choose this oscillator for high-speed applications requiring 50MHz clock frequency.
